title: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick
hints: Sort the cuts and include 0 and n as boundaries to define all segments.
This is a classic interval DP problem where you minimize cost by choosing the last cut in a range.

Frame a subproblem as an interval between two boundary positions with some cuts strictly inside it; solving it means choosing which interior cut to make first. Whichever you pick, that first cut costs the whole span between the two boundaries, and it splits the interval into two independent smaller intervals you solve the same way.
